what: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
text: Tools analyzing network-level functional connectivity fMRI data using parcellated brain atlases. Designed Schaefer atlas adaptable parcellation schemes. AHIP/PHIP connectivity 7 networks age group, generated based plot_compare outputs Hippocampal-Network connectivity vs. memory performance age group, generated based plot_scatter outputs

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
text: Install GitHub devtools: load connectivity matrices CONN toolbox .mat files, also need NetworkToolbox:
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("shijing-z/fcnet") install.packages("NetworkToolbox")

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
code: library(fcnet)  # Package includes example data: 30 ROIs x 10 subjects dim(ex_conn_array) #> [1] 30 30 10  # Organize ROIs by network indices <- get_indices(ex_conn_array)  # Network-level connectivity (Schaefer networks only) indices_sch <- get_indices(ex_conn_array, roi_include = "schaefer") # Results are data frames — one row per subject, one column per network within  <- calc_within(ex_conn_array, indices_sch) between <- calc_between(ex_conn_array, indices_sch)  # ROI-to-network connectivity ahip_df <- calc_conn(ex_conn_array, indices,                      from = "ahip", to = c("default", "cont", "vis"))  # Visualize plot_heatmap(ex_conn_array, indices)

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
text: examples show typical analysis pipeline. Replace file paths, subject indices, variable names .

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
text: Load connectivity matrices CONN toolbox output (requires NetworkToolbox): bring 3D array (ROI x ROI x subjects) ROI names dimnames.
code: # rmat has ROI names in CONN toolbox .mat files — use it for organizing ROIs r_mat <- load_matrices("data/conn.mat", type = "rmat")  # zmat (Fisher z-transformed) z_mat <- load_matrices("data/conn.mat", type = "zmat")  # Optional: exclude subjects by index # z_mat <- load_matrices("data/conn.mat", type = "zmat", exclude = c(3, 5))

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
code: # Extract ROI positions grouped by network indices <- get_indices(r_mat)  # Schaefer networks only (exclude custom ROIs) indices_sch <- get_indices(r_mat, roi_include = "schaefer")  # Group custom ROIs manually indices_hip <- get_indices(r_mat,   manual_assignments = list(ahip = "hippocampus", phip = "hippocampus"))

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
code: # Within-network: mean connectivity inside each network within_df <- calc_within(z_mat, indices_sch)  # Between-network: each network averaged across all other networks between_df <- calc_between(z_mat, indices_sch)  # Between-network: each unique network pair separately pairwise_df <- calc_between(z_mat, indices_sch, pairwise = TRUE)  # User-defined: any ROI/network to any target(s) ahip_df <- calc_conn(z_mat, indices,   from = "ahip", to = c("default", "cont", "limbic"))

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
text: plot functions return ggplot objects customization.
code: # Connectivity matrix heatmap plot_heatmap(z_mat, indices, subjects = demo$group == "YA", title = "Young Adults")  # Group comparison bar chart within_df <- cbind(within_df, group = demo$group) plot_compare(within_df,   conn_vars = c("within_default", "within_vis"),   group = "group")  # Connectivity-behavior scatter ahip_df <- cbind(ahip_df, demo[c("group", "memory")]) plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "memory", group = "group")

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
text: get_indices works Schaefer atlas version supports non-Schaefer ROIs. Attention network naming variations across atlas versions automatically normalized.

title: Functional Connectivity Analysis Tools for fMRI Data
text: MIT

title: Calculate between-network connectivity — calc_between
text: Calculate mean -network connectivity across subjects. Supports two modes: per-network averages (network vs. others) pairwise combinations (unique network pairs).

title: Calculate between-network connectivity — calc_between
code: calc_between(conn_array, indices, pairwise = FALSE)

title: Calculate between-network connectivity — calc_between
text: conn_array 3D numeric array connectivity values dimensions (ROI x ROI x subjects) indices Named list integer vectors mapping network names ROI index positions pairwise Logical. FALSE (default), returns per-network averages (network vs. networks). TRUE, returns unique pairwise combinations networks

title: Calculate between-network connectivity — calc_between
text: data frame one row per subject. pairwise = FALSE: one column per network (named between_{network}) between_network column overall -network average (raw cell average across -network connections). pairwise = TRUE: one column per unique network pair (named {net1}_{net2}).

title: Calculate between-network connectivity — calc_between
text: Entries indices fewer 2 ROIs automatically dropped warning, -network calculations network level intended multi-ROI networks. Use calc_conn single-ROI--network connectivity. overall between_network average (pairwise = FALSE) uses raw cell average: -network connections pooled averaged equal weight per cell. means larger network pairs contribute proportionally smaller pairs.

title: Calculate between-network connectivity — calc_between
Calculate user-defined connectivity — calc_conn
text: General-purpose connectivity calculator user-defined ROI network connections. Computes mean connectivity source () one targets () using raw cell average.

title: Calculate user-defined connectivity — calc_conn
code: calc_conn(conn_array, indices, from, to)

title: Calculate user-defined connectivity — calc_conn
text: conn_array 3D numeric array connectivity values dimensions (ROI x ROI x subjects) indices Named list integer vectors mapping network/ROI names index positions Character. Name source ROI network. Must match name indices Character vector. One target names. must match name indices. Connectivity calculated target separately

title: Calculate user-defined connectivity — calc_conn
text: data frame one row per subject one column per target, named {}_{}.

title: Calculate user-defined connectivity — calc_conn
text: Connectivity computed using raw cell average: subject, function extracts conn_array[from_idx, to_idx, subj] takes mean values. resolve single ROIs, direct connection value returned (averaging needed). Supports combination ROIs networks: ROI--network: e.g., = "ahip", = "default" ROI--ROI: e.g., = "ahip", = "phip" Network--network: e.g., = "default", = "cont" Names reference entries indices, including manual groupings.

title: Calculate user-defined connectivity — calc_conn
code: # ROI-to-network indices <- get_indices(ex_conn_array) ahip_default <- calc_conn(ex_conn_array, indices,                           from = "ahip", to = "default")  # ROI-to-multiple-networks ahip_nets <- calc_conn(ex_conn_array, indices,                        from = "ahip",                        to = c("default", "cont", "vis"))  # ROI-to-ROI ahip_phip <- calc_conn(ex_conn_array, indices,                        from = "ahip", to = "phip")  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Full workflow with manual grouping z_mat <- load_matrices("data/conn.mat", type = "zmat", exclude = c(3, 5)) indices <- get_indices(z_mat,   manual_assignments = list(ahip = "hippocampus", phip = "hippocampus"))  hip_nets <- calc_conn(z_mat, indices,                       from = "hippocampus",                       to = c("default", "cont", "limbic",                              "salventattn", "dorsattn", "sommot", "vis")) } # }

title: Calculate within-network connectivity — calc_within
text: Calculate mean within-network connectivity network across subjects. subject, extracts upper triangle network's submatrix (excluding diagonal) averages values.

title: Calculate within-network connectivity — calc_within
code: calc_within(conn_array, indices)

title: Calculate within-network connectivity — calc_within
text: conn_array 3D numeric array connectivity values dimensions (ROI x ROI x subjects) indices Named list integer vectors mapping network names ROI index positions

title: Calculate within-network connectivity — calc_within
text: data frame one row per subject. Contains one column per network (named within_{network}) within_network column average across networks.

title: Calculate within-network connectivity — calc_within
text: Entries indices fewer 2 ROIs automatically dropped warning, within-network connectivity requires least 2 ROIs compute upper triangle. Use calc_conn single-ROI connectivity.

title: Calculate within-network connectivity — calc_within

what: fcnet: Functional Connectivity Network Analysis — fcnet-package
title: fcnet: Functional Connectivity Network Analysis — fcnet-package
text: Tools analyzing network-level functional connectivity fMRI data. Provides functions loading connectivity matrices, organizing ROIs network, computing within-network, -network, user-defined connectivity, generating visualizations.

title: fcnet: Functional Connectivity Network Analysis — fcnet-package
text: load_matrices loads correlation z-transformed matrices CONN toolbox .mat files.

title: fcnet: Functional Connectivity Network Analysis — fcnet-package
text: get_indices extracts organizes ROI positions functional network. Supports Schaefer atlases (100-1000 parcels, 7 17 networks) custom ROIs.

title: fcnet: Functional Connectivity Network Analysis — fcnet-package
text: calc_within computes within-network connectivity. calc_between computes -network connectivity. calc_conn computes user-defined ROI network connectivity.

title: fcnet: Functional Connectivity Network Analysis — fcnet-package
text: plot_heatmap displays connectivity matrices heatmaps network boundaries. plot_compare creates group comparison bar plots. plot_scatter creates connectivity-behavior scatter plots. visualization functions return ggplot objects customization.

title: fcnet: Functional Connectivity Network Analysis — fcnet-package
text: functions work Schaefer atlas version support non-Schaefer ROIs hippocampal subregions.

title: fcnet: Functional Connectivity Network Analysis — fcnet-package
text: Maintainer: Shijing Zhou shijingz@uoregon.edu (ORCID)

what: Get ROI indices organized by network — get_indices
title: Get ROI indices organized by network — get_indices
text: Extracts ROI index positions organizes network region name. Schaefer ROIs automatically grouped functional network. ROIs preserved individually unless grouped via manual_assignments.

title: Get ROI indices organized by network — get_indices
code: get_indices(   x,   roi_include = c("all", "schaefer"),   reorder_networks = TRUE,   manual_assignments = NULL )

title: Get ROI indices organized by network — get_indices
text: x Input source ROI names. Can : file path CONN toolbox .mat file (requires NetworkToolbox) 3D connectivity array ROI names dimnames character vector ROI names roi_include Character. "" (default) processes ROIs; "schaefer" processes Schaefer-labeled ROIs. reorder_networks Logical. TRUE (default), reorder Schaefer networks : default, cont, limbic, salventattn, dorsattn, sommot, vis. Non-Schaefer ROIs always appear end. manual_assignments Named list override groupings. Format: list(roi_name = "group_name"). Matching case-insensitive.

title: Get ROI indices organized by network — get_indices
text: named list names network/ROI labels values integer vectors ROI indices.

title: Get ROI indices organized by network — get_indices
text: Schaefer ROIs grouped network (e.g., visual ROIs become "vis"). Non-Schaefer ROIs preserved individually lowercase names (e.g., "AHIP" becomes "ahip"). Attention network naming variations across atlas versions (ventatt, salventattn, dorsatt, etc.) automatically normalized consistency.

title: Get ROI indices organized by network — get_indices
code: # From character vector of ROI names indices <- get_indices(ex_roi_names) names(indices) #> [1] "default"     "cont"        "limbic"      "salventattn" "dorsattn"    #> [6] "sommot"      "vis"         "ahip"        "phip"         # Access specific network indices indices$vis #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5 indices$default #> [1] 25 26 27 28 indices$ahip #> [1] 29  # From connectivity array with dimnames indices <- get_indices(ex_conn_array) names(indices) #> [1] "default"     "cont"        "limbic"      "salventattn" "dorsattn"    #> [6] "sommot"      "vis"         "ahip"        "phip"         # Extract Schaefer ROIs only indices_schaefer <- get_indices(ex_roi_names, roi_include = "schaefer") names(indices_schaefer) #> [1] "default"     "cont"        "limbic"      "salventattn" "dorsattn"    #> [6] "sommot"      "vis"          # Manual grouping of ROIs indices_grouped <- get_indices(   ex_roi_names,   manual_assignments = list(ahip = "hippocampus", phip = "hippocampus") ) indices_grouped$hippocampus #> [1] 29 30  # Preserve original matrix order indices_original <- get_indices(ex_roi_names, reorder_networks = FALSE)  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # From CONN .mat file indices <- get_indices("path/to/conn.mat")  # From connectivity array loaded with NetworkToolbox conn_mats <- NetworkToolbox::convertConnBrainMat("path/to/conn.mat") indices <- get_indices(conn_mats$rmat) } # }

what: Load connectivity matrices from MATLAB file — load_matrices
title: Load connectivity matrices from MATLAB file — load_matrices
text: Load connectivity matrices CONN toolbox output. Extracts either correlation-based (rmat) Fisher z-transformed (zmat) connectivity matrices.

title: Load connectivity matrices from MATLAB file — load_matrices
code: load_matrices(mat_file, type, exclude = NULL)

title: Load connectivity matrices from MATLAB file — load_matrices
text: mat_file Character. Path MATLAB .mat file containing connectivity data. type Character. Type matrix: "rmat" (correlation) "zmat" (Fisher z-transformed). Must specified. exclude Integer vector. Subject indices exclude, NULL exclusions. Default NULL.

title: Load connectivity matrices from MATLAB file — load_matrices
text: 3D numeric array connectivity values dimensions (ROI x ROI x subjects). First two dimensions represent pairwise ROI connectivity, third dimension indexes subjects.

title: Load connectivity matrices from MATLAB file — load_matrices
code: # Example connectivity array dim(ex_conn_array) #> [1] 30 30 10 dimnames(ex_conn_array)[[1]] #>  [1] "Schaefer100.l_vis_1"                    #>  [2] "Schaefer100.l_vis_2"                    #>  [3] "Schaefer100.l_vis_3"                    #>  [4] "Schaefer100.r_vis_1"                    #>  [5] "Schaefer100.r_vis_2"                    #>  [6] "Schaefer100.l_sommot_1"                 #>  [7] "Schaefer100.l_sommot_2"                 #>  [8] "Schaefer100.r_sommot_1"                 #>  [9] "Schaefer100.r_sommot_2"                 #> [10] "Schaefer100.l_dorsattn_post_1"          #> [11] "Schaefer100.l_dorsattn_post_2"          #> [12] "Schaefer100.r_dorsattn_post_1"          #> [13] "Schaefer100.r_dorsattn_post_2"          #> [14] "Schaefer100.l_salventattn_paroper_1"    #> [15] "Schaefer100.l_salventattn_froperins_1"  #> [16] "Schaefer100.r_salventattn_tempoccpar_1" #> [17] "Schaefer100.r_salventattn_froperins_1"  #> [18] "Schaefer100.l_limbic_ofc_1"             #> [19] "Schaefer100.l_limbic_temppole_1"        #> [20] "Schaefer100.r_limbic_ofc_1"             #> [21] "Schaefer100.l_cont_par_1"               #> [22] "Schaefer100.l_cont_pfcl_1"              #> [23] "Schaefer100.r_cont_par_1"               #> [24] "Schaefer100.r_cont_pfcl_1"              #> [25] "Schaefer100.l_default_temp_1"           #> [26] "Schaefer100.l_default_par_1"            #> [27] "Schaefer100.r_default_temp_1"           #> [28] "Schaefer100.r_default_par_1"            #> [29] "AHIP"                                   #> [30] "PHIP"                                    # Use with get_indices() indices <- get_indices(ex_conn_array) names(indices) #> [1] "default"     "cont"        "limbic"      "salventattn" "dorsattn"    #> [6] "sommot"      "vis"         "ahip"        "phip"         # Extract specific network connectivity vis_connectivity <- ex_conn_array[indices$vis, indices$vis, ] dim(vis_connectivity) #> [1]  5  5 10  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Load z-transformed matrices with subject exclusions z_mat <- load_matrices(   mat_file = "data/conn.mat",   type = "zmat",   exclude = c(3, 5) )  # Load correlation matrices r_mat <- load_matrices(   mat_file = "data/conn.mat",   type = "rmat" )  # Check dimensions dim(z_mat)  # Combine with get_indices() indices <- get_indices(r_mat) vis_connectivity <- r_mat[indices$vis, indices$vis, ] } # }

what: Plot group comparison bar chart — plot_compare
title: Plot group comparison bar chart — plot_compare
text: Generate grouped bar plots error bars comparing connectivity metrics groups.

title: Plot group comparison bar chart — plot_compare
code: plot_compare(   data,   conn_vars,   group,   error_bar = c("se", "sd", "ci", "none"),   show_zero = TRUE,   clean_labels = TRUE,   title = NULL )

title: Plot group comparison bar chart — plot_compare
text: data Data frame one row per subject containing connectivity values grouping column conn_vars Character vector column names data compare. becomes category x-axis group Character string naming grouping column data. Must factor character column 2 levels error_bar Character. Type error bar display. One : "se" (default): standard error mean "sd": standard deviation "ci": 95 percent confidence interval "none": error bars show_zero Logical. TRUE (default), draw dotted horizontal reference line y = 0 clean_labels Logical. TRUE (default), clean column names x-axis display replacing underscores hyphens applying title case. FALSE, use raw column names title Optional character string plot title

title: Plot group comparison bar chart — plot_compare
text: ggplot object can customized standard ggplot2 syntax.

title: Plot group comparison bar chart — plot_compare
text: function computes per-group summary statistics (mean error bars) input data frame, generates grouped bar plot one cluster per connectivity variable. function works column names, limited calc_* output. numeric columns data frame can used conn_vars. X-axis labels can always overridden + scale_x_discrete(labels = ...) regardless clean_labels setting.

title: Plot group comparison bar chart — plot_compare
code: # Within-network comparison by group indices <- get_indices(ex_conn_array, roi_include = "schaefer") within_df <- calc_within(ex_conn_array, indices) within_df$group <- rep(c("YA", "OA"), times = c(5, 5))  plot_compare(within_df,   conn_vars = c("within_default", "within_vis"),   group = "group" )   # ROI-to-network comparison indices_all <- get_indices(ex_conn_array) ahip_df <- calc_conn(ex_conn_array, indices_all,   from = "ahip", to = c("default", "cont", "vis") ) ahip_df$group <- rep(c("YA", "OA"), times = c(5, 5))  plot_compare(ahip_df,   conn_vars = c("ahip_default", "ahip_cont", "ahip_vis"),   group = "group" )   # Custom labels override clean_labels plot_compare(within_df,   conn_vars = c("within_default", "within_vis"),   group = "group" ) + ggplot2::scale_x_discrete(labels = c("DMN", "VIS")) #> Scale for x is already present. #> Adding another scale for x, which will replace the existing scale.   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Full workflow with real data z_mat <- load_matrices("data/conn.mat", type = "zmat", exclude = c(3, 5)) indices <- get_indices(z_mat, roi_include = "schaefer") within_df <- calc_within(z_mat, indices) within_df$group <- demo$group  plot_compare(within_df,   conn_vars = c("within_default", "within_cont", "within_vis"),   group = "group",   title = "Within-Network Connectivity by Age Group" ) } # }

title: Plot connectivity matrix heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: Visualize group-averaged ROI x ROI connectivity matrix heatmap network boundary lines network-level axis labels. Averages across selected subjects displays network structure indices.

title: Plot connectivity matrix heatmap — plot_heatmap
code: plot_heatmap(   conn_array,   indices,   subjects = NULL,   diag = c("blank", "lower", "upper"),   grid = FALSE,   title = NULL )

title: Plot connectivity matrix heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: conn_array 3D numeric array connectivity values dimensions (ROI x ROI x subjects) indices Named list integer vectors mapping network names ROI index positions subjects Integer logical vector subset subjects (third dimension). Defaults subjects. Logical vectors converted integer indices internally diag Character. Controls diagonal triangle display. One : "blank" (default): set diagonal NA "lower": show lower triangle "upper": show upper triangle grid Logical. TRUE, draw network boundary lines edge lines heatmap. Defaults FALSE clean base plot easy customize additional ggplot2 layers title Optional character string plot title

title: Plot connectivity matrix heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: ggplot object can customized standard ggplot2 syntax.

title: Plot connectivity matrix heatmap — plot_heatmap
text: function averages connectivity values across selected subjects, plots resulting matrix using ggplot2::geom_tile() diverging color scale centered zero. Network boundary lines axis labels derived indices object. ROIs reordered match network ordering indices. ROIs appear heatmap controlled indices. Use roi_include get_indices include exclude non-Schaefer ROIs. y-axis reversed (scale_y_reverse()) row 1 appears top, matching conventional matrix layout. Keep mind adding custom scale layers, replacing y-scale flip orientation.

title: Plot connectivity matrix heatmap — plot_heatmap
code: # Basic heatmap of all subjects indices <- get_indices(ex_conn_array) plot_heatmap(ex_conn_array, indices)   # Subset subjects by index plot_heatmap(ex_conn_array, indices, subjects = 1:5)   # Subset with logical vector plot_heatmap(ex_conn_array, indices, subjects = c(rep(TRUE, 5), rep(FALSE, 5)))   # Lower triangle only plot_heatmap(ex_conn_array, indices, diag = "lower", title = "Lower Triangle")   # With boundary lines plot_heatmap(ex_conn_array, indices, grid = TRUE)   # Schaefer-only heatmap (exclude non-Schaefer ROIs) indices_sch <- get_indices(ex_conn_array, roi_include = "schaefer") plot_heatmap(ex_conn_array, indices_sch)   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Group comparison workflow z_mat <- load_matrices("data/conn.mat", type = "zmat", exclude = c(3, 5)) indices <- get_indices(z_mat)  # Young adults plot_heatmap(z_mat, indices, subjects = demo$group == "YA",              title = "Young Adults")  # Older adults plot_heatmap(z_mat, indices, subjects = demo$group == "OA",              title = "Older Adults") } # }

title: Plot connectivity-behavior scatter — plot_scatter
text: Create scatter plots optional linear regression lines exploring connectivity-behavior relationships. Points, regression lines, confidence bands can toggled , appearance can customized list arguments.

title: Plot connectivity-behavior scatter — plot_scatter
code: plot_scatter(   data,   x,   y,   group = NULL,   colors = NULL,   show_points = TRUE,   show_line = TRUE,   point_args = list(),   line_args = list(),   band_args = list(),   clean_labels = TRUE,   title = NULL )

title: Plot connectivity-behavior scatter — plot_scatter
text: data Data frame one row per subject containing connectivity behavioral/outcome columns x Character string naming x-axis column (typically connectivity variable) y Character string naming y-axis column (typically behavioral outcome variable) group Optional character string naming grouping column. provided, maps groups color. NULL (default), plots single set geoms colors Optional color specification. group provided, named character vector mapping group levels colors (e.g., c("YA" = "steelblue", "OA" = "coral")). group NULL, single color string applied geoms (e.g., "steelblue") show_points Logical. TRUE (default), display scatter points show_line Logical. TRUE (default), display linear regression lines confidence bands point_args Named list additional arguments passed geom_point. Overrides defaults (alpha = 0.6). See Details line_args Named list additional arguments passed geom_smooth controlling regression line. Overrides defaults (linewidth = 1). See Details band_args Named list additional arguments controlling confidence band. Supports se (logical, default TRUE), level (numeric, default 0.95), alpha (numeric, default 0.2). See Details clean_labels Logical. TRUE (default), clean column names axis display replacing underscores hyphens applying title case. FALSE, use raw column names title Optional character string plot title

title: Plot connectivity-behavior scatter — plot_scatter
text: ggplot object can customized standard ggplot2 syntax.

title: Plot connectivity-behavior scatter — plot_scatter
text: Regression lines fitted geom_smooth(method = "lm"). group provided, separate lines fitted group. least one show_points show_line must TRUE. point_args, line_args, band_args lists merged internal defaults using utils::modifyList(). Defaults: point_args: list(alpha = 0.6) line_args: list(linewidth = 1) band_args: list(se = TRUE, alpha = 0.2,     level = 0.95) Common point_args values include size, shape, alpha, color. See geom_point options. Common line_args values include linewidth, linetype, color, method (e.g., "loess"). See geom_smooth options. Band parameters se, level, alpha must passed band_args, line_args. found line_args, ignored warning. colors convenience shortcut applies uniform color layers. per-layer control, use color fill inside *_args lists instead. specified, *_args takes precedence colors. grouped plots points bands need different shades per group, override scales directly standard ggplot2 syntax: Axis labels can always overridden + labs(x = ..., y = ...) regardless clean_labels setting.
code: plot_scatter(df, x = "x", y = "y", group = "group") +   scale_color_manual(values = c("YA" = "navy", "OA" = "darkred")) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("YA" = "lightblue", "OA" = "lightsalmon"))

title: Plot connectivity-behavior scatter — plot_scatter
code: # Ungrouped scatter indices <- get_indices(ex_conn_array) ahip_df <- calc_conn(ex_conn_array, indices,   from = "ahip", to = "default" ) ahip_df$behavior <- rnorm(10)  plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior") #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'   # Ungrouped with a single color plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior",   colors = "steelblue" ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'   # Grouped scatter ahip_df$group <- rep(c("YA", "OA"), times = c(5, 5)) plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior",   group = "group" ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'   # Custom group colors plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior",   group = "group",   colors = c("YA" = "steelblue", "OA" = "coral") ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'   # Lines and bands only (no points) plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior",   group = "group",   show_points = FALSE,   colors = c("YA" = "steelblue", "OA" = "coral") ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'   # Points only (no line or band) plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior",   group = "group",   show_line = FALSE,   point_args = list(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) )   # Custom line style and wider confidence band plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior",   group = "group",   line_args = list(linetype = "dashed"),   band_args = list(alpha = 0.3, level = 0.99) ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'   # Disable confidence band plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior",   group = "group",   band_args = list(se = FALSE) ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'   # Different colors for points, line, and band plot_scatter(ahip_df, x = "ahip_default", y = "behavior",   point_args = list(color = "steelblue"),   line_args = list(color = "coral", fill = "grey80") ) #> `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Full workflow with real data z_mat <- load_matrices("data/conn.mat", type = "zmat", exclude = c(3, 5)) indices <- get_indices(z_mat) ahip_nets <- calc_conn(z_mat, indices,   from = "ahip", to = c("default", "cont")) df <- cbind(ahip_nets, demo[c("group", "memory")])  plot_scatter(df, x = "ahip_default", y = "memory", group = "group",   colors = c("YA" = "steelblue", "OA" = "coral"),   point_args = list(size = 2, shape = 17),   title = "AHIP-Default Connectivity on Memory" ) } # }
